7.1.title: मय्यासक्तमनाः — the locative of devotion
7.1.body: मय्यासक्तमनाः is a बहुव्रीहि — "one whose mind (मनस्) is fastened (आसक्त) in me (मयि सप्तमी)". The सप्तमी मयि locates the object of attachment, not a place; Krishna opens chapter 7 by naming the precondition for the knowledge to follow.

7.1.title: ज्ञास्यसि — लृट् promising the result
7.1.body: ज्ञास्यसि (√ज्ञा, लृट्, मध्यम, एकवचन — note the -स्य- infix) is the future Krishna stakes against Arjuna's listening: "you shall know". समग्रम् and असंशयम् are predicate-adverbial accusatives qualifying that knowing — wholly, without doubt.

7.2.title: सविज्ञानम् — saha-compound flagged by स-
7.2.body: सविज्ञानम् is an avyayībhāva-style बहुव्रीहि with the prefix स- meaning सह: "together with विज्ञान". Krishna offers not ज्ञान alone but ज्ञान-plus-experiential-realisation, अशेषतः (अव्यय, "without remainder").

7.2.title: ज्ञातव्यम् — the कृत्य participle of finality
7.2.body: ज्ञातव्यम् is a कृत्य (gerundive) from √ज्ञा — "that which is to be known". The claim यज्ज्ञात्वा... नान्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते uses the absolutive ज्ञात्वा to fold all further objects-of-knowing into this one act.

7.3.title: सहस्रेषु — सप्तमी of selection
7.3.body: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु uses सप्तमी बहुवचन to mark the pool from which the rare one (कश्चित्) is selected. यतति (√यत्, लट्, प्रथम, एकवचन) is आत्मनेपद-flavoured "strives", with सिद्धये (चतुर्थी) as the goal-dative.

7.3.title: तत्त्वतः — the अव्यय of accurate knowing
7.3.body: तत्त्वतः is a तसिल्-affixed indeclinable ("as-it-truly-is") qualifying वेत्ति. The verse stacks rarities: of thousands, few strive; among those who succeeded (सिद्धानाम् षष्ठी), still scarcer is the one who knows Krishna तत्त्वतः.

7.4.title: भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा — the eightfold cleavage
7.4.body: भिन्ना is the past passive participle of √भिद् agreeing with प्रकृतिः (स्त्रीलिंग, प्रथमा एकवचन). अष्टधा is an अव्यय in -धा meaning "in eight ways"; the eight items (भूमि through अहङ्कार) are all प्रथमा एकवचन, listed paratactically before being summed up by इयम् मे प्रकृतिः.

7.4.title: अहङ्कार — द्वन्द्व closed with इति
7.4.body: The whole list functions as a long द्वन्द्व, terminated by इति ("thus"), then resumed by the demonstrative इयम्. मे is षष्ठी एकवचन ("of mine"), claiming the eightfold prakṛti as Krishna's lower nature.

7.5.title: अपरा / परा — the comparative pair of natures
7.5.body: अपरा ("lower") and पराम् ("higher") are both feminine adjectives agreeing with प्रकृतिम् (द्वितीया एकवचन). इतस्तु अन्याम् sets the second prakṛti in deictic contrast to the first: "other than this".

7.5.title: जीवभूताम् — the कृदन्त bridging matter and life
7.5.body: जीवभूताम् is a तत्पुरुष whose second member भूता is the past participle of √भू — "become-jīva", "having become the jīva". यया... धार्यते is लट् कर्मणि (passive) with the तृतीया relative — "by which this world is upheld".

7.6.title: एतद्योनीनि — बहुव्रीहि naming the source
7.6.body: एतद्योनीनि is a बहुव्रीहि (नपुंसकलिंग, प्रथमा बहुवचन agreeing with भूतानि): "those whose womb (योनि) is this (एतद्)". The compound packs the cosmology — the two prakṛtis are the gestation site of all beings.

7.6.title: प्रभवः प्रलयः — paired -अ nouns of origination and dissolution
7.6.body: प्रभवः and प्रलयः are both प्रथमा एकवचन predicate nouns equated with अहम् by implicit अस्मि. कृत्स्नस्य जगतः is षष्ठी एकवचन dependent on both — Krishna claims the entire cycle, not one phase.

7.7.title: मत्तः परतरम् — पञ्चमी of comparison
7.7.body: मत्तः is the पञ्चमी एकवचन of अहम् ("than me"), licensed by the comparative परतरम् (तरप्-affixed adjective). The pile-up नान्यत् किञ्चित् अस्ति makes the exclusion absolute — nothing whatsoever stands beyond Krishna.

7.7.title: सूत्रे मणिगणा इव — the locative simile of thread and beads
7.7.body: मयि... प्रोतम् is a लट् कर्मणि-style past participle (√प्रोत from प्र+वे) — "strung in me". The simile सूत्रे मणिगणा इव keeps the same सप्तमी ("as bead-clusters on a thread"), so the syntax of the metaphor mirrors the syntax of the claim.

7.8.title: रसः... प्रणवः — the predicate-nominative refrain
7.8.body: Each item — रसः, प्रभा, प्रणवः, शब्दः, पौरुषम् — is प्रथमा एकवचन predicated of अहम्/अस्मि. The locatives अप्सु, शशिसूर्ययोः (द्विवचन!), सर्ववेदेषु, खे, नृषु locate the manifestation without dissolving Krishna into it.

7.8.title: शशिसूर्ययोः — द्विवचन preserved in द्वन्द्व
7.8.body: शशिसूर्ययोः is a द्वन्द्व compound in सप्तमी द्विवचन — moon-and-sun as a natural pair. प्रभा ("radiance") is what Krishna is in both luminaries; the compound's grammatical duality matches the cosmological pairing.

7.9.title: पुण्यो गन्धः — adjective resolving the fragrance
7.9.body: पुण्यः agrees with गन्धः (पुल्लिंग, प्रथमा एकवचन) — "the holy fragrance in the earth". The locatives पृथिव्याम्, विभावसौ (in fire), सर्वभूतेषु, तपस्विषु chain four habitats; अस्मि is gapped after the first and supplied by the listener.

7.9.title: तपस्तपस्विषु — figura etymologica
7.9.body: तपः and तपस्विषु share the root तप्; the noun is the abstract, the locative names the practitioners. Krishna is the very austerity inside the austere — the cognate pairing tightens the claim into self-reference.

7.10.title: बीजं... सनातनम् — the eternal seed
7.10.body: बीजम् is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन (object of विद्धि लोट्); सनातनम् agrees with it. माम् is the apposed accusative — "know me as the eternal seed of all beings".

7.10.title: बुद्धिमताम् / तेजस्विनाम् — षष्ठी of possessor groups
7.10.body: बुद्धिमताम् and तेजस्विनाम् are षष्ठी बहुवचन of मतुप्/विनि-affixed adjectives. Krishna is the बुद्धि *of* the intelligent and the तेजस् *of* the lustrous — not a quality among them but the genitive source they belong to.

7.11.title: कामरागविवर्जितम् — three-member तत्पुरुष
7.11.body: कामरागविवर्जितम् is a तृतीया-तत्पुरुष: "abandoned-by (विवर्जित, ppp) कामराग (काम-and-राग, द्वन्द्व)". It agrees with बलम् — Krishna is the strength of the strong only when stripped of lust and passion.

7.11.title: धर्माविरुद्धः कामः — adjective licensing desire
7.11.body: धर्माविरुद्धः is a बहुव्रीहि ("that which is not opposed to dharma") qualifying कामः (पुल्लिंग, प्रथमा एकवचन). भूतेषु is सप्तमी बहुवचन — within beings, the desire that does not contradict dharma is itself Krishna.

7.12.title: मत्तः एव — पञ्चमी of origination
7.12.body: मत्त एव uses the same पञ्चमी एकवचन as in 7.7, but here for source rather than comparison: the सात्त्विक, राजस, and तामस भावाः all proceed *from* Krishna. भावाः is प्रथमा बहुवचन with two adjective-groups apposed.

7.12.title: न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि — the asymmetric locatives
7.12.body: The closing pair flips containment: तेषु ("in them") negated, मयि ("in me") affirmed. Krishna is not contained by the guṇa-states; the guṇa-states are contained by him — sapta-mī पुरुष on either side, but only one direction is true.

7.13.title: त्रिभिर्गुणमयैः — तृतीया of bewildering instrument
7.13.body: त्रिभिः, गुणमयैः, एभिः, भावैः form a single तृतीया बहुवचन phrase (all पुल्लिंग to agree with भावैः). गुणमय is the मयट्-affixed adjective "consisting of guṇas". The instrumental tells *by what* the world is bewildered.

7.13.title: मोहितम् — past participle hiding the verb
7.13.body: मोहितम् (ppp of √मुह् causative) agrees with जगत् (नपुंसकलिंग); the finite verb is नाभिजानाति (नञ् + अभि+√ज्ञा, लट्). Note the एभ्यः परम् — पञ्चमी of comparison again: Krishna is *beyond* these guṇa-states.

7.14.title: दैवी... गुणमयी मम माया — three feminines locking together
7.14.body: दैवी, गुणमयी, and दुरत्यया are all स्त्रीलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with माया. मम is षष्ठी एकवचन possessor. The grammatical agreement enforces the claim: this māyā belongs to Krishna and shares his divinity.

7.14.title: प्रपद्यन्ते — आत्मनेपद of surrender
7.14.body: प्रपद्यन्ते is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन आत्मनेपद of प्र+√पद् — the आत्मनेपद flavour fits surrender (action returning to the agent). तरन्ति (परस्मैपद, √तॄ) is the consequence; the contrast of pada-set marks surrender versus crossing-over.

7.15.title: मायया अपहृतज्ञानाः — instrumental robbing the participle
7.15.body: मायया is तृतीया एकवचन, the instrument by which ज्ञान is stolen (अपहृत, ppp of अप+√हृ). अपहृतज्ञानाः is a बहुव्रीहि — "those whose knowledge has been stolen" — apposed to नराधमाः.

7.15.title: आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः — absolutive logic inside the participle
7.15.body: आश्रिताः is ppp of आ+√श्रि ("having taken refuge in"), governing आसुरं भावम् (द्वितीया एकवचन). Krishna lists four reasons the wicked do not approach: दुष्कृति, मूढता, ज्ञानहरण, and resort to the आसुर mode — each phrase a participial epithet.

7.16.title: चतुर्विधाः — समाहार-द्विगु breaking into four
7.16.body: चतुर्विधाः is the सङ्ख्या-तत्पुरुष / द्विगु adjective "of four kinds" (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन with जनाः). The four are then listed in प्रथमा एकवचन — आर्तः, जिज्ञासुः, अर्थार्थी, ज्ञानी — each a distinct grammatical formation.

7.16.title: आर्त / जिज्ञासु / अर्थार्थिन् / ज्ञानिन् — four affix-types
7.16.body: आर्तः is ppp of √ऋ ("afflicted"); जिज्ञासुः is the desiderative-noun from सन्-formation of √ज्ञा; अर्थार्थी is एक्ता upapada-तत्पुरुष "seeking-wealth" (इन्-stem); ज्ञानी is also इन्-stem from ज्ञान. The four etymologies map four orientations of devotion.

7.17.title: नित्ययुक्तः एकभक्तिः — twin बहुव्रीहि epithets
7.17.body: नित्ययुक्तः ("ever-yoked") and एकभक्तिः ("of single devotion") are both पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन बहुव्रीहि compounds qualifying ज्ञानी. विशिष्यते is लट् कर्मणि — "is distinguished", passive of वि+√शिष्.

7.17.title: अहं प्रियः... स च मम प्रियः — mutual प्रिय
7.17.body: The verse stages reciprocal predication: ज्ञानिनः (षष्ठी) is dear to whom Krishna is dear; मम (षष्ठी again) holds the same construction. अत्यर्थम् is an अव्यय intensifier — "exceedingly dear". The mutual genitives are the grammatical core of the verse.

7.18.title: ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव — the identification beyond the list
7.18.body: उदाराः ("noble") groups all four devotee-types as प्रथमा बहुवचन, but ज्ञानी is then singled out and equated with आत्मा via एव. मे मतम् is the predicate construction "my view (मतम्, ppp of √मन्) is". The grammar steps from generosity (एते) to identity (आत्मा एव).

7.18.title: अनुत्तमां गतिम् — द्वितीया of attainment
7.18.body: अनुत्तमाम् is स्त्रीलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन agreeing with गतिम् — "the unsurpassed goal". माम् is the apposed accusative; आस्थितः (ppp of आ+√स्था) makes "established". Krishna is named both as person (माम्) and as destination (गतिम्).

7.19.title: बहूनां जन्मनाम् अन्ते — षष्ठी under सप्तमी-like अन्ते
7.19.body: बहूनां जन्मनाम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन dependent on अन्ते ("at the end of many births"). अन्ते is सप्तमी एकवचन of अन्तः and functions almost adverbially. The chronology is built entirely from case endings, no preposition needed.

7.19.title: वासुदेवः सर्वम् इति — the इति-clause of realisation
7.19.body: वासुदेवः सर्वम् is the content of the realisation, sealed by इति. महात्मा is a बहुव्रीहि ("of great soul"); सुदुर्लभः is a पञ्चमी-tatpuruṣa-flavoured adjective with intensifying सु- — "very-hard-to-obtain".

7.20.title: कामैस्तैस्तैः — distributive तृतीया
7.20.body: कामैस्तैस्तैः is तृतीया बहुवचन with reduplicated demonstrative तैस्तैः marking distribution — "by these and those various desires". हृतज्ञानाः is a बहुव्रीहि ("having-knowledge-stolen") parallel to 7.15's अपहृतज्ञानाः, but with the simpler हृत.

7.20.title: प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया — instrumental of compulsion
7.20.body: प्रकृत्या (तृतीया एकवचन) is the instrument; स्वया agrees with it as adjective; नियताः (ppp of नि+√यम्) is the participial predicate. The सम्बन्ध is: "compelled by their own prakṛti" — turning to lesser deities is grammatically constrained, not freely chosen.

7.21.title: यो यो यां याम् — full reduplication of relative pronouns
7.21.body: यः and याम् are reduplicated for distributive force — "whichever (devotee) wishes to worship whichever (form)". यः agrees with भक्तः (पुल्लिंग); याम् with तनुम् (स्त्रीलिंग द्वितीया). The doubled relatives establish a one-to-one open mapping.

7.21.title: अर्चितुम् — तुमुन् infinitive of intention
7.21.body: अर्चितुम् is the तुमुन्-affixed infinitive of √अर्च् completing इच्छति ("wishes to worship"). विदधामि (लट्, उत्तम, एकवचन of वि+√धा) is Krishna's response — *"that very (तामेव) faith I confer"* — the deictic एव binds confer to wish.

7.22.title: तया श्रद्धया युक्तः — instrumental + ppp
7.22.body: तया श्रद्धया (तृतीया एकवचन feminine) is the instrument of yoking; युक्तः is the ppp of √युज् agreeing with सः. ईहते (लट् आत्मनेपद, √ईह्) is the आत्मनेपद verb of striving, with तस्य (षष्ठी, of the chosen deity) and आराधनम् (द्वितीया, object).

7.22.title: मयैव विहितान् — instrumental + ppp claiming the gift
7.22.body: विहितान् is ppp of वि+√धा ("ordained") agreeing with कामान् (पुल्लिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन). मया is तृतीया एकवचन — *"those very desires-fulfilments, ordained by me alone"*. The एव in मयैव is the crucial restrictive particle: lesser gods are not the source.

7.23.title: अन्तवत् फलम् — मतुप्-affixed adjective of finitude
7.23.body: अन्तवत् is the मतुप्-affixed adjective "possessing-end" (नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with फलम्). It contrasts with the अव्यय quality Krishna claims for himself. अल्पमेधसाम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन — "of those of little wit" (a बहुव्रीहि).

7.23.title: देवयजः / मद्भक्ताः — upapada compounds of destination
7.23.body: देवयजः is उपपद-तत्पुरुष "deva-sacrificers" with क्विप्-affixed यज् as the verbal-root-noun; मद्भक्ताः is the corresponding self-compound "my-devotees". यान्ति (लट्, √या, परस्मैपद) governs both, but the destinations diverge — case-grammar staging two parallel sentences.

7.24.title: अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नम् — chiastic ppp of manifestation
7.24.body: अव्यक्तम् ("unmanifest") and व्यक्तिमापन्नम् ("having reached manifestation") are both नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन — apposed to माम्. आपन्नम् is ppp of आ+√पद् taking व्यक्तिम् (द्वितीया) as its sub-object. The unwise reverse the order of being.

7.24.title: अबुद्धयः — nañ-tatpuruṣa of those-without-discernment
7.24.body: अबुद्धयः is the नञ्-तत्पुरुष "non-buddhi-ones" (प्रथमा बहुवचन), subject of मन्यन्ते (लट्, आत्मनेपद, √मन्). परम् भावम् is द्वितीया एकवचन object of the negated शतृ-participle अजानन्तः ("not-knowing").

7.25.title: योगमायासमावृतः — three-member compound veiling Krishna
7.25.body: योगमायासमावृतः is a तृतीया-तत्पुरुष: "covered (समावृत, ppp of सम्+आ+√वृ) by yoga-māyā". The agent is grammatically dissolved into the compound — there is no separate तृतीया noun, the instrument is absorbed.

7.25.title: अजम् अव्ययम् — the un-born, un-decaying twin
7.25.body: अजम् and अव्ययम् are both नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन agreeing with माम् — though Krishna is male, the adjectives in pure-abstract usage often take नपुंसक. अज is a नञ्-तत्पुरुष "not-born", अव्यय is नञ्-tatpuruṣa "not-decaying".

7.26.title: वेदाहम् — perfect-stem present of √विद्
7.26.body: वेद is the लट्-like perfect (विद्-form) of √विद् — "I know", उत्तम, एकवचन. The verse stages three temporal कृदन्त-adjectives — समतीतानि (sam+ati+√इ, ppp "gone past"), वर्तमानानि (शानच् present participle of √वृत्), भविष्याणि (the future participle of √भू with the -ष्य- signature).

7.26.title: मां तु वेद न कश्चन — the inverted final line
7.26.body: Krishna's three-time-knowing is matched by the negative: *of me, however, knows no one* — माम् is द्वितीया एकवचन pulled to the front for emphasis, तु the contrastive अव्यय, कश्चन the indefinite. The grammatical asymmetry mirrors the metaphysical one.

7.27.title: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन — instrumental of compound origin
7.27.body: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन is a four-member compound in तृतीया एकवचन: "by the (द्वन्द्वमोह) arising-from desire-and-aversion". The inner इच्छाद्वेष is द्वन्द्व; समुत्थ is a कृत् noun "arisen-from" governing it. The whole tells *by what* delusion comes.

7.27.title: सर्गे यान्ति सम्मोहम् — locative of cosmogonic moment
7.27.body: सर्गे is सप्तमी एकवचन — "at creation / at emanation". यान्ति (लट्, √या) takes सम्मोहम् (द्वितीया एकवचन) as the destination — *"they go *to* delusion"* — turning a mental state into a place reached. परन्तप is सम्बोधन: vocative epithet.

7.28.title: येषाम्... अन्तगतम् पापम् — षष्ठी of those whose sin is ended
7.28.body: येषाम् is षष्ठी बहुवचन relative pronoun; अन्तगतम् is a बहुव्रीहि-flavoured ppp compound ("gone-to-end") agreeing with पापम् (नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन). The case structure: *those of whom sin has gone to its end*.

7.28.title: द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ताः दृढव्रताः — paired बहुव्रीहि epithets
7.28.body: द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ताः is पञ्चमी-tatpuruṣa with ppp निर्मुक्त ("freed-from द्वन्द्वमोह"); दृढव्रताः is बहुव्रीहि ("those whose vows are firm"). The two epithets together govern भजन्ते (लट् आत्मनेपद, प्र+√भज् without the prefix here).

7.29.title: जरामरणमोक्षाय — चतुर्थी of liberating purpose
7.29.body: जरामरणमोक्षाय is चतुर्थी एकवचन — the dative of purpose. The inner जरामरण is द्वन्द्व; मोक्ष takes it as षष्ठी inside the compound — "for liberation from old-age-and-death". माम् आश्रित्य is the absolutive (ल्यप्) phrase: *having taken refuge in me*, they then strive (यतन्ति).

7.29.title: ब्रह्म कृत्स्नम् अध्यात्मम् कर्म च — three objects of विदुः
7.29.body: तत् ब्रह्म, कृत्स्नम् अध्यात्मम्, कर्म च अखिलम् are three coordinated द्वितीया-style objects of विदुः (perfect-form, प्रथम बहुवचन of √विद्). कृत्स्नम् and अखिलम् are quantifier-adjectives in the same case, totalising the knowledge — Krishna links liberation-effort to total knowledge.

7.30.title: साधिभूताधिदैवम् साधियज्ञम् — saha-prefixed accusatives
7.30.body: साधिभूताधिदैवम् packs sa- + अधिभूत + अधिदैव (a द्वन्द्व); साधियज्ञम् sa- + अधियज्ञ. All are द्वितीया एकवचन apposed to माम् — *"me along with adhibhūta, adhidaiva, and adhiyajña"*. The prefix स- compresses three full clauses into two compounds.

7.30.title: प्रयाणकाले — सप्तमी of the final moment
7.30.body: प्रयाणकाले is सप्तमी एकवचन — "at the time of departure". The juxtaposition with अपि च ("even then") promises that grammatical knowledge of Krishna survives the death-moment. युक्तचेतसः is बहुव्रीहि "those whose mind (चेतस्) is yoked" — closing chapter 7 by echoing 7.1's मय्यासक्तमनाः.
